mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the INDPRO trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are not followed by higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO changes in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above a low-rate threshold marks the start of tighter discount-rate pressure, reducing the present value of future cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% after being at or below 0.50% within the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS threshold crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with falling unemployment identifies strengthening household income and consumption demand, increasing expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either single-series condition over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term has a nonpositive coefficient in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp unemployment-rate increase signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, causing earnings downgrades and risk-premium widening that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if triggered returns are not lower than nontriggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above their recent trend signal stronger construction activity, credit creation, and durable-goods demand, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds its trailing 12-month median by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-above-trend triggers fail to deliver positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if their hit rate for positive returns is not above the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices together with falling industrial production identifies stagflationary cost pressure, compressing margins while weakening real output and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and INDPRO-down state is not followed by below-average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP momentum signals broad demand expansion and higher aggregate profits, supporting equity earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 changes are not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative changes, or if the sign of the GDP coefficient is negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains combined with rising policy rates create a policy-reaction shock in which good labor news raises expected tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 300 thousand and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-strong and FEDFUNDS-rising trigger has nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or does not underperform strong-payroll periods without rising FEDFUNDS.
